transcript: Hello, and you're listening to KGNU Boulder, and... how are you guys tonight?
...very shortly we're going to be talking to Mark Hosler of Negativland.
'[sampled]' Glad you could all come over!
Let's go ahead and take our first caller...
People are just playing with the buttons out of control here.
How does that come through, anyway?
[distorted] How does that come through, anyway?
I think we have a caller on the line, if you'd like to take a question for a second here.
The problem is, Gibby, is that there's so much background to do.
Hello, do you have a question tonight, for Mark?
Hi there. Um... I've been listening to your band for a long time, and I've liked a whole lot of stuff you've done, all the way back to "Car Crash"...
"[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Escape_from_Noise Car Bomb]"! You got it wrong...
...so you must not be listening to us that closely.
Well... the question was, have you guys considered, um... re-releasing on CD all the work that you've done up to date?
Uh, what do you... what do you mean by that?
Um... the old... the old LPs...
Well, they are available on CD.
Okay, that's good to know. I didn't know that.
It is a testament to our own lack of aggressiveness of promoting our own work that you don't know about it.
And caller, you brought up a very good point, I'd like to add.
Uh, the conversion of, uh, handmade, uh... early recordings, now available on the popular CD...
Well, they're available on CD. And, uh--
Okay, so can I... can I order... should I order those through you?
Is that [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Over_the_Edge_Vol._5:_Crosley_Bendix:_The_Radio_Reviews Universal Media Netweb]? Is that the...?
What did... what did... what did [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Eno Eno] tell you?
You're supposed to have the magazine for when we do this interview. So you know what the hell you're talking about.
That's not part of the public domain, then, huh?
Well, actually, I believe that it is, it's ju... the... thing is...
...it's that these press releases, is not motivated by, um...
We want it to be a celebration, because we think that everything you're doing's really great.
What, uh... let's take our second caller... at this time. Hello, you're on the air, caller.
Hi Mark, I'm calling back again. Um... at the risk of...
...you being, um... angry at me for not having Venture Magazine...
Do you ever hear from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bono Bono]? Directly?

Uh, no, though he's actually floated... [https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/u2-finds-what-its-looking-for-189823/3/ Rolling Stone article] about our... about... a little bit about our record.
Okay. I couldn't understand why he would be upset about anything like that.
Well, that's what... I would think that he wouldn't even... like you say...
...if he's... if it's... well, why would he be upset about some...
What did... what did [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Eno|Eno] tell you?
Uh, Brian Eno is, uh, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U2 U2]'s producer, which... I'm a... a Brian Eno fan...
And, uh, I actually have always thought it was very interesting that he ended up being their producer.
Did you hear about the lawsuit against [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jeff_Koons Jeff Koons] in New York?
Oh yeah, we've been following that.
Okay. The... the final decision is not out, and this will be a continuing... we'll hear about this in the future, on a ongoing basis.
Yeah, and that's one reason why we felt that we should put out our magazine, was that we really wanted to sort of throw all our two cents into the debate here...
...and especially, we realized that, at least in the world of music, the people who are actually creatively using the technology in the world of media around them are never represented.
What about... uh, Mark, what about "[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guns_(EP) Guns]", for New American Radio? What... what... what...
[delayed] We already have a caller that... we're gonna take one more caller.
...that... that... you were parodying all the westerns, right? Because you...
I wouldn't say we were parodying anything there, no. I think we were just referencing...
...we were referencing, you know, just how... how much... uh... weapons like that are in our culture.
'[sampled, delayed]' --tell you one thing I hate, all the noise, noise, noise, noise!
[delayed] I'm back here with Mark Hosler. Uh, the caller we...
The caller we just had, Mark, wanted to know if you've...
...[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dweezil_Zappa Dweezil Zappa]. Or [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frank_Zappa Frank Zappa], rather, as he's, uh...
Don't have enough data! Mark, you there?
Yeah, I just got just a little... blues number now.
You're just, uh... returnin' it in spades, are ya?
[laughs] He's gonna return it in spades.
Yeah, we just had a caller who was wondering if you had, uh, contacted Frank Zappa, who, uh... has a lot to do with censorship, and things like that.
No, we haven't. Okay, now I'm going to do my impersonation of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gheorghe_Zamfir Gheorghe Zamfir] on the pan pipes.
Don't you feel ready to all go out and see that movie [https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0209919/ Aspen]?
You know, that's where... that's what people are going to think of Colorado, they'll look at that movie, that...
Isn't that the worst? Hello, caller, what do you have to say for yourself as a Colorado resident?
You tell them to put the tape recorder closer to the phone so that it'll come through.
Somebody doin' that with half your brain, type...
...the... the Little... the Little Dipper's, uh... placement tonight. Maybe, uh... that caller will call back.
Well, they'll... they'll cut him off, if they're do... you know, that...
Oh, they did. Oh, I'm sorry.
Hey Mark, I got kind of a technical question, you... well... uh...
[slurring] Well, I don't know about... about anything technical...
[slurring] Well, I don't know! What do you want to ask me?
How come there's no "E" in your name?

'[sampled, looping]' Glad you could all come over! Oh, boy! [chuckles] Glad you could-- Glad you could-- Glad you could all come over! Now, are you ready to-- ready to-- ready to-- ready to-- ready to-- ready to-- ready to--
Oh. The joys of a CD...
...a CD player that you can just...
Here's someone who's really not... for copyright infringement, and that's [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walt_Disney Walt Disney].
Who did he rip? Who did he borrow from?
'[sampled, pitch shifting]' [unclear] ...there's a theme that I use, here... [unclear]
Well, I don't know, I guess he was sort of... did a lot of terrible things to...
...shh... uh, I don't know, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Igor_Stravinsky Stravinsky] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dmitri_Shostakovich Shostakovich] and, uh... lots of people...
And, uh, caller, anything you wanted to add?
'[sampled]' Okay, what's your number? I'll call you back. I ain't got time right now, I'm trying to hang sausage.
Oh, alright, what do you want?
Trying to hang sausage, are ya? Okay, now, see, what, uh... what's your take on that?
Hey, come on, you're... you're, see, you're... you're... you're... you're ig... you're just ignoring those calls that are actually good calls. You know, I can hear that those are good calls.
They just keep hanging up, really.
Let's talk about your new label!
Can we be on your new label?
I don't know, send us some tapes. We don't really want to put anyone else on our new label except ourselves.
You... you could have the money.
Hi, I'm here. Uh, uh... I'm a fanzine artist and frankly, I'm scared by all this. Uh...
A lot of fanzine artists clip out little samples of, you know, in... in the sound...
...you know, and uh... I want to...
Look what happened to us! We're not scared!
I want to... yeah, I want to congratulate you for fighting the good fight for, you know... you're doing the...
[laughs] I mean, it sounds corny, but... true. Um... and also for exposing... you know, these quote-unquote "underground" labels. You know, uh...

...many of these undergrounds just call themselves... now, such as [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SST_Records SST] network... uh, you know... tons of underground labels are making millions of dollars, and, uh....
Yes, the story is the same, it's just a different... has a different face to it.
Yeah. Well, uh... you know, the whole fanzine world, you know...

...basically cuts up lots of things and puts them in their art, and it's not, like, a whole... clipping out of a whole magazine. You know, like, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FedEx_Office Kinko's] copy center got sued a while back for that same kind of copyright infringement.
They traced it back to there, huh?
I think that must have been done to make an example of them...
...or to scare the shit out of them.
I just said "shit". I didn't say... I just said "shit".
What do you... what do you think of caller ID?
I think all that stuff's very frightening.
But unfortunately, this is what I'd have to say, is that... I'd say, keep on clipping out your art and doing what you do. I mean, there's... there's a lot of people who are, uh...

...how many rap... or rather, hip hop... uh... that... they're not all getting clearance fees, and they're not all playing the game. They somehow act like they are, and they say they are... but they, uh...
Um... they're just keeping quiet about it.
Yeah. Is your magazine... completely unavailable now? Or...
It is now completely unavailable, that is correct.
And, uh, you know... that last caller... uhh... it looks like he was looking for a little encouragement tonight from you. And, uh... do you think you can offer it, uh, genuinely this evening, or...?

I just think that the smaller you are, I think that... I think if you're just doing a Xeroxed fanzine, I mean, it... I just wouldn't worry about it. And even when you're doing bigger stuff, I wouldn't worry about it. And, uh... I would worry about it, only if and when something actually happens.
'[sampled]' I'd always wear... fingernail polish to match my outfit.
'[sampled]' If I wore a yellow outfit, I'd wear yellow fingernail polish. And that was a ritual I did day after day.
Oh, I'm wearing... pairs of long underwear right now.
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanna_White Vanna White]... Vanna White's gonna sue us... right now, I think.
[delayed, pitch-shifted] I'm gonna sue you, that's what I'm gonna do... and, um... Mark Hosler, always a pleasure.
Hey, this is No-One, Number Two.
It's... I got... I got it right over here.
I got it... I got it, ready? Ready?
Okay, we've obviously got some aquatic listeners this evening.
Now, we're going to move on to, uh... a compilation that D.U. Records, uh... released.

style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |We would like to thank... thank you too, for comin' by.

style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |Play more of that phone call stuff so I don't have to stay on the phone and make obscene calls all night.
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;; background: #141315; color: #B090C5;" |[delayed] Okay, I'm afraid that's all there is for tonight...
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;; background: #141315; color: #B090C5;" |[delayed] ...but, uh, we do appreciate your call.
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;; background: #141315; color: #B090C5;" |[delayed, pitch-shifted] Um...
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |'[delayed]' Well, why don't you do one for us?
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |Maybe you guys could call her, get her number, and...
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |Do you have Prince Albert in a can?
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;; background: #141315; color: #B090C5;" |Okay. And I do want to thank you for you call. Um. And, uh...
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |'[delayed, pitch-shifted]' Thanks for putting up with our little offering this morning. It was really fun.
style="width: 88%; text-align:left;" |Always glad to have creative folks along on the ride through the night time airwaves.
